rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JOHN CURTSINGER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF PROVIDED EVIDENCE THROUGH THE DECLARATIONS OF AY AND GLADDEN THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. CHARLES M. COLLINS et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 2, PLAINTIFF PAUL VANDER WAERDT'S Motion To Continue The Trial Date IS OFF CALENDAR, TO BE RE-NOTICED IN DEPARTMENT 206. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD BOVEE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 19, DEFENDANT ROSENDIN ELECTRIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE FROM DEFENDANT AS TO WHAT IT KNEW. THE BIRKNER DECLARATION, ASSUMING IT IS ADMISSIBLE FAILS TO DO SO. PARAGRAPHS 2 AND 3 OF THE BIRKNER DECLARATION DO NOT SET FORTH SUFFICIENT FOUNDATIONAL FACTS FOR THE COURT TO FIND THAT HE IS QUALIFIED TO GIVE EACH OF THE OPINIONS EXPRESSED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GENEVA R. SPARKS VS. ROGER WADE ESTEY JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANTS ROGER ESTEY, JULIAN CHANG, AND TAMSEN PLUME'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THEY WERE BONA FIDE PURCHASERS WHO ACQUIRED THE PROPERTY WITHOUT ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. DEFENDANTS' DECLARATIONS FROM PRIOR OWNERS CONFIRMS DEFENDANTS LACK OF KNOWLEDGE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. CIVIL CODE SECTION 19, SOUTHERN PACIFIC CO. V. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (1964) 62 CAL.2ND 50, 56-57; IN RE MARRIAGE OF CLONEY (2001) 91 CAL.APP.4TH 429, 437. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: TIM FORD VS. BENNETT S BIBEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bennett Bibel'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Motion To Stay Action And Attorney'S Fees Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 21, Defendant Bennett Bibel's Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Motion To Stay Action And Attorney's Fees Costs. THE MOTION IS DENIED BASED ON THE EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION UNDER CCP SECTION 1281.2(C). THE LITIGATION HAS BEEN GOING FOR 16 MONTHS. PETITIONER HAS SOUGHT INDEMNIFICATION FROM MCGUIRE AND IT IS NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. GIVEN PETITIONERS DECISION TO BRING MCGUIRE INTO THE CASE, IT MAKES SENSE TO RESOLVE ALL OF THE ISSUES IN ONE PROCEEDING. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD THOMAS VS. SEAN MILLIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Advanced From 12/11/08) Per Agreement Of The Parties
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 8, 2009, line 14, Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment IS GRANTED.
